requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must rent personal residence or vacation home for 14 days or fewer during tax year to receive tax-free rental income without reporting income or depreciation deductions under IRC §280A(g) Augusta Rule exception
criteria: Rent dwelling unit (home, vacation property, apartment) used as personal residence
Total rental period cannot exceed 14 days (or 10% of days rented at fair rental value if longer, whichever greater)
Rental income completely tax-free - no reporting on tax return required
Cannot deduct rental expenses (depreciation, utilities, maintenance) allocable to rental period
Fair rental value determination important if renting to related party or own business
Personal mortgage interest and property taxes remain deductible as normally allowed
Strategy works for business owners renting home to own business for meetings, retreats, events
Document rental arrangement, business purpose, fair market rental rate, and actual rental days

augusta_rule_requirements.14_day_limit: Rental period cannot exceed 14 days; 15th day triggers full rental income reporting
augusta_rule_requirements.tax_free_income: Rental income not reported; completely excluded from gross income under §280A(g)
augusta_rule_requirements.no_rental_deductions: Cannot deduct expenses allocable to rental period (no depreciation, utilities, cleaning)
augusta_rule_requirements.personal_deductions_preserved: Mortgage interest and property taxes remain deductible as itemized deductions
augusta_rule_requirements.business_use_planning: Business owners can rent home to own business for legitimate meetings and events
augusta_rule_requirements.fair_rental_value: Must charge fair market rental rate, especially for related party transactions

restrictions: Renting 15+ days disqualifies Augusta Rule; must report all rental income and follow vacation home rules
IRS scrutinizes business rentals to related entities; must establish legitimate business purpose and FMV rent
Cannot create loss by deducting rental expenses; if disqualified, vacation home passive loss limitations apply
Fair market value determination critical; compare to similar venue rentals in area
Must document actual rental use, business purpose, attendees, agenda, and business benefit
Sham transaction risk if no legitimate business purpose or rental rate unreasonably high
State tax treatment generally follows federal but verify state conformity to Augusta Rule exclusion
Property must be dwelling unit; cannot use rule for office buildings or pure commercial property
